description: THE NATIVE JAZZ QUARTET is a hard-swinging and high energy group based on the swing/bebop tradition using indigenous and folk melodies. The NJQ was co-founded by Ed Littlefield (Lingít - drums) and Christian Fabian in 2011. Featured in the band is longtime member Reuel Lubag on piano, Michael Glynn on bass and Delbert Anderson (Diné) on Trumpet. Everyone in the band also contributes arrangements of melodies that come from their distinct heritage. The goal of the trio is to grow the Native Jazz movement and to attract a national and international audience of all ages. The Native Jazz Quartet is an exciting band, with an exciting message, that turns performances into cultural stories. In person. Refunds up to 7 days before event.

description: Historic Dance Workshop. Learn to dance three beautiful social dances: polka, waltz, menuet. It’s a chance to step into another era for an evening — to move, laugh, meet new people, and experience the joy of traditional ballroom dancing in a relaxed, welcoming setting. No experience needed. Just curiosity and a willingness to try. You’ll learn real steps, dance with others, and leave knowing something you didn’t know before. It’s social, elegant, and surprisingly fun. ---- And as a bonus — this is connected to the main event the Vancouver Spring Gala Ball . So if you enjoyed the workshop, please check out the Gala Ball Event. The Gala Ball is a participatory European-style evening where everyone dances together. Elegant, welcoming, and just a little magical. Beginner-friendly. Solo guests and couples welcome. Come learn, move, and see how it feels. ---- 🔗 Learn More & Stay Connected 🌐 Website: https://nocturnegala.wixsite.com/spring-ball 📘 Facebook: www.facebook.com/nocturnegala 📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nocturnegala/

description: Van Django is a standout gypsy jazz act with a clever mix of standards, classical ditties, rock homages and solid originals! "Van Django is a standout act. With a clever mix of contemporary standards, classical ditties, rock homages and solid originals they effortlessly whip the audience into a joyful frenzy. Attending a Van Django concert is the most fun you can have sitting down with your clothes on." - NICK LEHR, Artistic Director, Djangofest Northwest Van Django is an acoustic string ensemble made up of four of Canada's most talented and eclectic musicians; violinist Cameron Wilson , guitarist Budge Schachte , guitarist/cellist Finn Manniche and bassist Brent Gubbels . Van Django's music is well-rooted in the gypsy jazz of 1930's Paris France, but beyond that it's all pure blue-skying; flights of dazzling fancy, driving rhythms and boundless creativity featuring original compositions and arrangements including humorous mash-ups that showcase their eclectic influences in full regalia. Since the group's formation in 1998, they have toured extensively in Canada as well as international forays to the USA, Europe and China, they've had repeat performances at Djangofest Northwest (DFNW) on Whidbey Island. They have released 3 CDs "Tiptoe Trip", "Waltz in the Shape of a Tree" and a Christmas CD with special guests titled, "Cool Yule". Their CD's have been well received and played on many radio stations at home and abroad including; CBC, RDI, Espace Musique, NPR and Lyric FM in Ireland. www.vandjango.com Please note the Upstairs Lounge is an all ages licensed restaurant and bar, doors open at 6 pm for dinner service, the concert starts at 7:30 pm (this is a listening room).
location: Oak Bay Recreation - Personal Training/Active Rehab, 1975 Bee, Victoria, BC, CA

description: Jazz, Blues and the sweet notes in between Maureen Washington is the Winner of the 2014 Black Canadian Award for Best Jazz and Soul Artist! With all splendor attached to such comparisons, Maureen’s musical style remains distinctly her own. Her vocal diversity lends itself to performances of blues, swing jazz and funk , and her versatile repertoire, ranges from classics to lively originals.

description: The band offers the energetic stage show they are renowned for as they cover a varied catalogue of songs that fill the dance floor. A Beatles band with a slightly different take. Their focus is on the Beatles from Hamburg, to the Cavern, and the BBC studios- the songs they wrote, the songs they covered and the songs they loved. They play early period songs, with a nod to the more well-known tracks. They cover many of the Beatles seminal influences, from early rock and roll classics like Chuck Berry’s Memphis, to the slinky R&B of Anna and on to the country and rockabilly vein of I Got a Woman (their version of the Beatles doing Elvis’s cover of a Ray Charles song). When the Sutcliffes were invited to play The Liverpool Beatles Festival they realized the dream of every kid who ever listened to the Beatles. The annual event sees tens of thousands of visitors make the pilgrimage to listen to some of the greatest performers from around the world. The Sutcliffes were the first artists from B.C. to ever be invited and were there with over 50 other acts from Europe, Asia and North and South America. The Sutcliffes are a band that is in high demand, they have a huge following of devoted fans, so be sure to get your tickets early to avoid disappointment. www.sutcliffesband.com The Upstairs Lounge is a licensed restaurant and bar, doors open at 6 pm for dinner service, the band starts playing at 7:30 pm. Please note tickets are NO LONGER AVAILABLE through the Oak Bay Recreation Centre. If you wish to purchase tickets directly from us please call Marie at 250-812-7978.
location: Oak Bay Recreation - Personal Training/Active Rehab, 1975 Bee, Victoria, BC, CA

event_name: Spring Kickoff! Latin Night Boat Party
description: Join us for a high-energy Latin Night Boat Party featuring the best reggaeton, bachata, Latin hits, and vibrant dance floors. Get ready to turn up the heat at Vancouver's Biggest Latin Night Boat Party! This event is filled with the best reggaeton, bachata, and Latin hits, vibrant dance floors, and stunning views of the city skyline. Grab a drink, feel the rhythm, and dance the night away as we cruise into the new season. The Abitibi vessel offers a unique multi-level experience, featuring an indoor salon and spacious outdoor/indoor decks transformed into nightclub environments. Our DJs will spin the hottest tracks from Top 40, Hip Hop, and more, creating an electrifying atmosphere under the stars. Highlights include: Capacity for 300+ guests, 4-hour Party, Live DJ's, Multiple dance floors, Two full bars, Food available for purchase, Smoking is permitted on designated outdoor decks. Event Details: Boarding: 7:00 PM, Gate closes: 7:55 PM, Sailing: 8:00 PM, Finish: 11:00 PM, 19+ Event. Venue: Abitibi, Address: Dock B, Pacific Boulevard Marina, 750 Pacific Boulevard, Vancouver, British Columbia. Whether you're celebrating with friends or just looking for a fun night out, this is the place to be. Dress to impress, bring your energy, and prepare for a party that will have you dancing until the very last beat. Ticket Policy: No Refunds.
location: 750 Pacific, Vancouver, BC V6B 5E7, CA
